findings.summary: Fixed engagement fees barely move with scope or duration: the longest, most senior programs are priced at roughly half the monthly rate of the shortest ones, inverting how professional-services fees usually scale.
findings.evidence: Ledger fees (engagements.json) cluster tightly regardless of term: Corporate Positioning $59,000, Investor Relations Program $47,000, Crisis Communications $46,500, Media Relations $46,000, Reputation Audit $45,500, with Executive Messaging $102,000 the lone outlier. The letters state the terms and teams: d:0018 'runs approximately nine months' as a partner-led IR program (Michael Morris, Partner, plus a senior associate and an analyst) at $47,000, about $5,200/month; d:0038 'runs approximately eight months' of crisis communications at $46,500, about $5,800/month; while d:0061 'runs about five months' at $102,000, about $20,000/month. A nine-month partner-led IR program and an eight-month crisis engagement priced below a four-month reputation audit, and crisis work (the premium line) billed at ~$5,800/month, read as a single default project price rather than scope-based pricing to a reader who knows the market. (Distinct from the report's fee-to-revenue metric, which addresses the aggregate sample gap, not per-engagement pricing.)
